data.content:
# Implementation plan: rip out compat-status from Foolery run paths

## Scope clarification
"Execution-plan run paths" here means the run-time execution code paths
(bd/knots create/update/close pipelines), not the `execution_plan` knot
type. The `orchestration-plan-*` modules were verified by grep to have
zero direct references to compat-status.

## Current state (inventory, 2026-04-17)
Compat-status abstraction lives in three shared modules and four beads-
only consumer modules:

Shared (leaks out of beads scope):
- `src/lib/workflows-runtime.ts` — `mapWorkflowStateToCompatStatus`
  (lines 142-170), `mapStatusToDefaultWorkflowState` (173-190),
  `WorkflowRuntimeState.compatStatus` field (369, 389),
  `deriveWorkflowState` calls `mapStatusToDefaultWorkflowState` (326).
- `src/lib/workflows.ts` — re-exports both mapping functions (464-465).
- `src/lib/compat-status-usage.ts` — telemetry counters.
- `src/lib/knots-compat.ts` — `mapKnotsStateToFooleryStatus` (69),
  `mapFooleryStatusToKnotsState` (79); ADR-locked per file header.

Beads-only consumers (live run paths):
- `src/lib/memory-manager-commands.ts:57` — `bd update` status flag.
- `src/lib/bd-update.ts:83, 103-107, 127` — resolve status in update.
- `src/lib/bd-mutations.ts:54, 63-68` — derive status on create.
- `src/lib/backends/beads-jsonl-dto.ts:210` — JSONL denormalize.
- `src/lib/backends/beads-backend.ts:313` — close() state mapping.

Tests:
- `src/lib/__tests__/compat-status-usage-coverage.test.ts` — telemetry.
- `src/lib/__tests__/knots-compat.test.ts` — compat mapping.
- `src/lib/__tests__/workflows-coverage-labels.test.ts:15-16, 123-243`.
- `src/lib/__tests__/workflows-coverage-inference.test.ts:269-285`.
- `src/lib/__tests__/memory-manager-commands-coverage.test.ts:76`.
- `src/lib/__tests__/mock-backend-port.ts:26, 157, 224`.

## Non-beads status check
Beads autorouting is still live (see `backend-factory.ts:47-48`) — the
beads backend is selected when `detectMemoryManagerType(repoPath)` is
`beads`. So the run paths using compat-status are still exercised.
Conclusion: compat-status is legitimate migration debt *inside the
beads backend*, but must not exist in shared modules, on
`WorkflowRuntimeState`, or in the knots-facing surface.

## Implementation phases

### Phase A — contain compat-status to beads-only scope
A1. Create `src/lib/backends/beads-compat-status.ts`. Move
    `mapWorkflowStateToCompatStatus` and
    `mapStatusToDefaultWorkflowState` from `workflows-runtime.ts` into
    it. Remove the re-exports from `workflows.ts`.
A2. Update beads consumers (`bd-update.ts`, `bd-mutations.ts`,
    `memory-manager-commands.ts`, `beads-jsonl-dto.ts`,
    `beads-backend.ts`) to import from the new module path.
A3. Remove the `compatStatus` field from `WorkflowRuntimeState`
    (`workflows-runtime.ts:366-374`). Consumers
    (`bd-update.ts:103-107`, `bd-mutations.ts:63-67`) replace
    `deriveWorkflowRuntimeState(...).compatStatus` with a direct
    `mapWorkflowStateToCompatStatus(state, ctx)` call.
A4. Remove `mapStatusToDefaultWorkflowState` call from
    `deriveWorkflowState` (`workflows-runtime.ts:311-329`). The only
    caller passing a legacy `status` is `deriveBeadsWorkflowState` —
    move the `mapStatusToDefaultWorkflowState` step into that beads-
    specific helper and leave the generic `deriveWorkflowState` for
    label-/descriptor-native derivation only.

### Phase B — remove dead compat surface
B1. `src/lib/compat-status-usage.ts`: telemetry was observational; with
    the layer contained to beads, delete the module and all
    `recordCompatStatusSerialized` / `recordCompatStatusConsumed`
    calls. Delete `compat-status-usage-coverage.test.ts`.
B2. `src/lib/knots-compat.ts`: the two mapping functions have zero
    runtime callers (only referenced by their own test file). Delete
    them and delete `__tests__/knots-compat.test.ts`. Keep the edge
    kinds, close target, metadata keys, and capability flags — rename
    the file to `src/lib/knots-constants.ts` for clarity (and update
    imports).

### Phase C — tests & doc alignment
C1. Prune compat-status assertions:
    - `workflows-coverage-labels.test.ts:15-16, 123-243` — move tests
      of `mapWorkflowStateToCompatStatus` /
      `mapStatusToDefaultWorkflowState` into a new
      `__tests__/beads-compat-status.test.ts` that targets the beads
      module directly.
    - `workflows-coverage-inference.test.ts:269-285` — remove
      `compatStatus` field assertions.
    - `mock-backend-port.ts:26, 157, 224` — replace
      `mapStatusToDefaultWorkflowState(...)` calls with direct state
      literals from the test fixture workflow descriptor.
C2. Add a workflow-state test covering `deriveWorkflowState` with no
    `status` fallback, proving the shared function is fully label/
    descriptor-driven.
C3. Update `docs/adr-knots-compatibility.md` to note: "compat-status
    mappings are scoped to the beads backend only; shared workflow
    code does not expose them." (ADR narrowed, not superseded.)

## Test strategy
- After each phase: `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`,
  `bun run build` (per CLAUDE.md quality gates).
- Acceptance-mapped verification:
  1. Grep `src/lib` (excluding `src/lib/backends/beads-*` and the new
     `beads-compat-status.ts`) for `compat` / `compatStatus` —
     should return zero hits.
  2. Readback: on a beads-flavored test repo, confirm `bd update`
     commands still emit the correct `--status` flag after the move.
  3. Readback: on a knots-flavored test repo, confirm no compat-status
     call is reachable on the knots path (existing
     `memory-manager-commands.ts:53-56` branch is already clean;
     verify the assertion holds after A4).

## Risks
- **R1**: Silent regression in beads run paths after relocating
  mapping functions. Mitigation: keep function bodies identical; the
  move is mechanical, and beads-specific tests (relocated in C1)
  exercise them directly.
- **R2**: `WorkflowRuntimeState.compatStatus` is part of an exported
  interface — downstream consumers outside the beads modules could
  break. Grep confirms only `bd-update.ts` + `bd-mutations.ts` read
  it today, both of which we update in A3.
- **R3**: ADR lock on `knots-compat.ts` mappings. Mitigation: the
  mapping tables cover knots-simple-state→foolery-status, which has
  no live callers; removing them aligns with ripping out the
  translation layer per knot constraint. Update the ADR doc to
  narrow scope.

## Complexity
Medium. ~10 files touched in Foolery; no new public surface added;
no cross-cutting behavior change — the net effect is relocating
beads-specific logic into the beads backend and deleting 2-3
unused-in-production modules.

## Deliverables
- `src/lib/backends/beads-compat-status.ts` (new; lifted from
  `workflows-runtime.ts`).
- `src/lib/knots-constants.ts` (renamed from `knots-compat.ts`, with
  the two compat mapping functions deleted).
- Deletions: `src/lib/compat-status-usage.ts`,
  `__tests__/compat-status-usage-coverage.test.ts`,
  `__tests__/knots-compat.test.ts`.
- Edits: `workflows.ts`, `workflows-runtime.ts`,
  `memory-manager-commands.ts`, `bd-update.ts`, `bd-mutations.ts`,
  `backends/beads-jsonl-dto.ts`, `backends/beads-backend.ts`,
  `__tests__/workflows-coverage-labels.test.ts`,
  `__tests__/workflows-coverage-inference.test.ts`,
  `__tests__/mock-backend-port.ts`,
  `docs/adr-knots-compatibility.md`.
- New: `__tests__/beads-compat-status.test.ts`.

## Invariant adherence
No invariants listed on this knot. Plan respects the knot constraints:
(a) no replacement generic translation layer is introduced; (b)
direct workflow-aware logic replaces hidden remapping on the
shared/knots paths.
